gloss_on_first_occurrence: kāyagatā sati (sati directed to the body)
definition: A mode of sati grounded in careful attention to the body as a field of practice and understanding.
notes: The project keeps kāyagatā sati untranslated because the phrase is a specific technical expression, and shorter English options tend to flatten either the role of sati or the force of the compound. A concise gloss on first occurrence is usually enough.

context_rules.context: In source-facing translation and technical practice discussion
context_rules.notes: Default project practice is to keep the phrase untranslated.

context_rules.context: On first occurrence where a gloss is needed
context_rules.notes: Use the gloss 'kāyagatā sati (sati directed to the body)' on first occurrence.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where the structure of the phrase is being unpacked
context_rules.notes: Acceptable when a compact English gloss is necessary.

example_phrases.translation: through kāyagatā sati one becomes beyond training
example_phrases.notes: Shows the phrase as a technical practice expression.

example_phrases.translation: when kāyagatā sati is developed and cultivated
example_phrases.notes: Strong example of the phrase in practice instructions.
